requiredConfiguration: Create MIDI Preset ArrangerLab in the first empty slot; do not overwrite presets
Channels 1/2/3 = Upper 1/2/3, channel 4 = Lower, channel 16 = Control
Allow required CC, PC and only known SysEx
For the partDamper verification, select Sound mode with the ArrangerLab preset so channel 1 routes directly to Upper 1
Use External USB clock only during the arranger Start/Stop experiment; restore Internal afterward

evidence.note: Captured from Pa700 KEYBOARD after universal identity inquiry to Pa700 SOUND
Outgoing CC7 on configured channel 1 at normalized level 0.25
Outgoing CC7 on configured channel 1 at normalized level 0.50
Outgoing CC7 on configured channel 1 at normalized level 0.75

ArrangerLab preset physically confirmed. RMS -52.23, -47.27, -43.38 dBFS; 8.85 dB total and strictly increasing. User confirmed audible change. Canonical evidence: Experiment-2026-07-15T13-44-05Z.arrlab in Application Support.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 850 documents Expression as CC11 relative to the current CC7 volume
Outgoing CC11 on configured channel 1 at normalized level 0.25
Outgoing CC11 on configured channel 1 at normalized level 0.50
Outgoing CC11 on configured channel 1 at normalized level 0.75

ArrangerLab preset physically confirmed. With CC7 fixed at 75%, RMS -62.46, -59.90, -54.72 dBFS; 7.75 dB total and strictly increasing. User confirmed the 25% level was audibly very low. Canonical evidence: Part-Expression-2026-07-16T00-37-03Z.arrlab in Application Support.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 pages 433 and 881 document CC10 Pan Pot: 0 or 1 far left, 64 oscillator center, 127 far right
Outgoing CC10 on configured channel 1 at far left
Outgoing CC10 on configured channel 1 at center
Outgoing CC10 on configured channel 1 at far right

ArrangerLab preset and PA700 identity physically confirmed. User confirmed center, left and right stereo positions; the app restored center after every clip. Mono microphone RMS -51.44, -50.16 and -50.02 dBFS is retained as an audit trail, not directional proof. Canonical evidence: Part-Pan-2026-07-16T00-50-15Z.arrlab in Application Support.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 pages 230 and 292 document Damper (Hold 1) as CC64
Outgoing CC64 OFF on configured channel 1 before and after the comparison
Outgoing CC64 ON on configured channel 1 before the sustained note

With PA700 in Sound mode and MIDI preset 14 ArrangerLab, channel 1 mapped directly to Upper 1, the user confirmed the dry first note and the sustained second note. Damper was restored OFF. The physical damper jack was disconnected. Canonical evidence: Part-Damper-2026-07-16T02-36-54Z.arrlab in Application Support.

devicePreset.template: Bn 00 msb; Bn 20 lsb; Cn pc

evidence.note: Classic Piano A1 and A2 Bank Select MSB captured from Upper1 channel 1
Classic Piano A1 and A2 Bank Select LSB captured from Upper1 channel 1
Classic Piano A1 and A2 Program Change captured from Upper1 channel 1

User confirmed Classic Piano. A1/A2 both 121.4.0; contrast Jimmy Organ 121.13.18. Averaged spectral distances: A1-A2 0.078398, A1-B 0.386705, A2-B 0.417488. Canonical evidence: Preset-ABA-2026-07-15T17-01-13Z.arrlab in Application Support.
Grand Piano A1 and A2 Bank Select LSB captured from Upper1 channel 1; CC0=121 and PC=0 completed both canonical selections

User confirmed Grand Piano. A1/A2 both 121.3.0; contrast Jimmy Organ 121.13.18. Spectral distances: A1-A2 0.1674, A1-B 0.2887, A2-B 0.1831. Canonical evidence: Preset-ABA-2026-07-16T04-47-08Z.arrlab in Application Support; the earlier marginal Draft remains preserved.

styleSelection.template: Control: Bn 00 msb; Bn 20 lsb; Cn pc

evidence.note: Korg PA700 User Manual v1.5 Musical Resources pages 942-947 lists all 379 factory Styles and their exact CC00, CC32 and PC values on the special Control channel.
Arranger Lab sent Bossa Nova as CC0=0, CC32=6, PC=2 on configured Control channel 16. The user physically confirmed that Style selection and rhythms work on the PA700.

The PA700 v1.5 Musical Resources table defines User Styles as CC0=2 with CC32=bank 0...11 and PC=slot 0...47. Arranger Lab sent JPD (User bank 11, slot 1) as CC0=2, CC32=10, PC=0 on configured Control channel 16 after first selecting Brush Ballad; the user physically confirmed that the PA700 returned to JPD.
Universal realtime Master Coarse Tuning value 61 selected transpose -3; user physically confirmed the PA700 display changed to -3.

keyboardSetLibrarySelection.template: Control: Bn 00 msb; Bn 20 lsb; Cn pc

evidence.note: Korg PA700 User Manual v1.5 Musical Resources pages 948-952 lists all 298 factory Keyboard Sets and their exact CC00, CC32 and PC values on the special Control channel.
Arranger Lab sent Jimmy Organ as CC0=16, CC32=1, PC=0 on configured Control channel 16. The user physically confirmed that Jimmy Organ appeared on the PA700 display.
One Start FA followed by 2010 Clock F8 messages at 120 BPM; PA700 Arranger physically confirmed started in Style Play with Clock Source External USB
Stop FC plus Panic produced three FC, 32 All Notes Off and 32 All Sound Off events; PA700 Arranger physically confirmed stopped
41.91-second mono 48 kHz WAV captured. User confirmed Start, Stop and restoration of Clock Source Internal. Canonical evidence: Arranger-Transport-2026-07-15T17-27-58Z.arrlab in Application Support.

midiClock.template: FA/FB/FC transport; F8 at 24 PPQN by the transport scheduler

evidence.note: The verified Arranger transport experiment emitted 2010 MIDI Clock F8 pulses at 120 BPM after Start FA

PA700 physically followed External USB clock; the user confirmed Start and Stop, and Clock Source was restored to Internal. Canonical evidence: Arranger-Transport-2026-07-15T17-27-58Z.arrlab in Application Support.

songBook.template: Control: Bn 63 02; Bn 62 40; Bn 06 hh; Bn 26 ll

evidence.note: SongBook NRPN initialization on configured Control channel 16; followed by BF 62 40
SongBook 9000 Data Entry MSB 90; followed by BF 26 00
User confirmed Style Play and supplied a panel photo showing SBook: ArrangerLab Te... after remote selection. Canonical evidence: SongBook-9000-2026-07-15T18-06-22Z.arrlab in Application Support.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Intro 1
CF 50 lit the physical Intro 1 indicator, but two early start attempts were inconclusive.
Controlled retest: Stop + Panic, CF 50, then verified CF 67 Start. User heard Intro 1 before the Variation and confirmed playback.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Intro 2
With the ArrangerLab MIDI Preset active and arranger stopped, CF 51 lit Intro 2. A verified CF 67 Start then played the intro before the Variation; user confirmed selection and playback.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 pages 58 and 884: Intro 3 / Count In

With the arranger physically confirmed stopped and the ArrangerLab MIDI Preset active, CF 52 lit the Intro 3 / Count In indicator. A subsequent CF 67 produced one audible count-in measure before Variation 1; user physically confirmed both selection and playback.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Variation 1
CF 53 selected Variation 1; user physically confirmed the Variation 1 panel indication with ArrangerLab preset selected
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Variation 2
With the persisted ArrangerLab MIDI Preset active and the USB session reconnected and identity-checked, CF 54 lit the physical Variation 2 indicator; user confirmed the panel state.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Variation 3
With the persisted ArrangerLab MIDI Preset active and the USB session reconnected and identity-checked, CF 55 lit the physical Variation 3 indicator; user confirmed the panel state.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Variation 4
With the persisted ArrangerLab MIDI Preset active and the USB session reconnected and identity-checked, CF 56 lit the physical Variation 4 indicator; user confirmed the panel state.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Fill 1
CF 57 armed the Fill while stopped; during internal-clock playback it produced an audible Fill and returned to the Variation. User confirmed playback.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Fill 2
CF 58 produced an audible Fill during internal-clock playback; user confirmed the musical transition.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Fill 3
CF 59 produced an audible Fill during internal-clock playback; user confirmed the musical transition.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Fill 4
CF 5A produced an audible Fill during internal-clock playback; user confirmed the musical transition.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Break
CF 5B produced an audible Break during internal-clock playback and the rhythm resumed; user confirmed the pause and return.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Ending 1
CF 5C played Ending 1 during internal-clock playback and stopped the arranger automatically; user confirmed both behavior and stop.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Ending 2
CF 5D played Ending 2 during internal-clock playback and stopped the arranger automatically; user confirmed the distinct ending and stop.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Ending 3
CF 5E played Ending 3 during internal-clock playback and stopped the arranger automatically; user confirmed the ending and stop.
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 885 documents Control-channel PC 64-67 for Keyboard Set 1-4 after a Style or SongBook selection
CF 41 selected SongBook Keyboard Set 2; user photo physically confirmed Kbd2 and displayed name Organ
CF 43 selected SongBook Keyboard Set 4; user photo physically confirmed Kbd4 and displayed name Harmonica
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Fade In/Out
With Brush Ballad and internal clock, CF 5F while stopped started a gradual Fade In; a second CF 5F during playback produced a gradual Fade Out and automatic stop; user physically confirmed both
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Style to Kbd Set
Three successive CF 60 sends cycled the physical STYLE TO KBD SET indicator from off to steady on to blinking and back to off; user confirmed every transition
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Auto Fill
Successive CF 61 sends turned the physical AUTO FILL indicator off and back on; user confirmed both states
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Memory
Successive CF 62 sends established MEMORY on and then off through its physical indicator; user confirmed both states
PA700 User Manual v1.5 pages 199 and 884: Bass Inversion
In Style Play > Split, successive CF 63 sends checked and unchecked the Bass Inversion control; user physically confirmed both states
PA700 User Manual v1.5 pages 66 and 884: Manual Bass

CF 64 left MANUAL BASS off without an active keyboard split; after the physical SPLIT indicator was on, successive CF 64 sends turned MANUAL BASS on and off; user confirmed the prerequisite and both states
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Tempo Lock
Successive CF 65 sends turned the physical TEMPO LOCK indicator on and off; user confirmed both states
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Arranger Start/Stop
First CF 67 started Brush Ballad at internal 73 BPM; second CF 67 stopped it; user physically confirmed both and Panic followed
PA700 User Manual v1.5 page 884: Player Play/Stop
With Song Play and the internal DISK song Late 1 selected, the first CF 68 started Player playback and the second CF 68 stopped it; user physically confirmed both transitions
A1 and A2 matched on Upper1 channel 1; followed by CC32=3 and PC=0

Grand Piano displayed-name confirmation and passing A-B-A spectral evidence in Preset-ABA-2026-07-16T04-47-08Z.arrlab. After rebuilding the app with the promoted profile, the simple Tocar page sent 121.3.0 and the PA700 remained on Grand Piano; the user physically confirmed the displayed result.
A1 and A2 matched on Upper1 channel 1; followed by CC32=4 and PC=0
Classic Piano displayed-name confirmation and passing A-B-A spectral evidence
A1 and A2 matched on Upper1 channel 1; followed by CC32=13 and PC=18

Jimmy Organ displayed-name confirmation, passing A-B-A spectral evidence in Preset-ABA-Jimmy-Organ-Verified-2026-07-16T03-51-36Z.arrlab, and final Classic Piano to Jimmy Organ selection through the simple Tocar page physically confirmed
